The table showed that the amount of warranty did not conform to the
required amount indicated in Section 62.2.3.3 of RA 9184 which is 30% of the
total project cost.
AOM No. 17-008 (16) dated March 8, 2017 was issued and received by
Management on March 16, 2017 for comments.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Treasurer opined that they did
not know about the difference between the type of warranties. They thought that
whatever type it is, it will always be 10%. But with this audit observation, they
will be more careful next time and abide by the regulation.
We recommended that the BAC require the contractor to issue another
warranty that is compliant to the provision of the IRR of RA 9184. Likewise, the
Municipal Accountant must see to it that the correct amount of warranty security
which is 30% of the total contract price be imposed if surety bond is used for the
same future transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive adhere strictly to Section
287 of RA 7160 and Joint DILG & DBM Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated
April 13, 2011 and ensure that only projects aligned with the purpose/objectives of
the 20% Development Fund be funded from such appropriation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere to Section 313 of
RA 7160 or the Local Government Code, and Section 111 of PD 1445. He should
likewise maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for special accounts in the GF for the
LGU’s 20% DF in order to have a readily adequate and reliable information as to the
object of expenditures of these projects as well as the financial status, and if these
were in line with the guidelines set forth under the 20% DF implementation.

We recommended that a meticulous, careful and judicious planning of
procurement activities be done by the BAC before the Approved Budget of the
Contract will be determined so as to arrive at a more reasonable price of goods.
Likewise, we recommended that in the selection of suppliers/contractors, the
BAC should be guided by a reasonable eligibility criterion to come up with eligible
and qualified suppliers for an effective and efficient procurement system.
We further recommended that Purchase Orders or Contracts with Suppliers
should state in specific dates, the date of delivery of procured goods, to afford
specific reckoning dates. Vague measure of time such as “immediately” should be
avoided.
Finally, the Municipal Accountant should require the payment of liquidated
damages, for every day of delay of goods procured, before payment will be made.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

c) Require the employees concerned to adhere strictly to Section 89 of P.D.
1445; COA Circular 97-002 specifically Sections 5.7 and 8 , and COA
Circular 2012-004 dtd. November 28, 2012 on the grant, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances. Failure of the Accountable Officer
concerned to liquidate cash advances within the period prescribed
hereunder and after formal demand shall constitute a prima facie case of
Gross Negligence of duty as defined under CSC Resolution No. 040676
dated 6/17/2014-Rule II, Section 4.

d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to accountable
officers who were granted cash advances for the liquidation thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local Treasurer
and Accountant to observe strictly COA Circular No. 2012-001 and that all fund
transfers must have a Memorandum of Agreement to include a provision that the
infrastructure project must be validated upon completion of the project through the
preparation of an inspection report by the Municipal Engineer based on the statement
of work accomplished prepared by the school to be attached to the liquidation report.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the strict observance to
the provisions of Section 272 and 100 of the LGC, Article 363 of its Implementing
Rules and Regulations and Item 4 of Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 of the DECS,
DBM and DILG, in the utilization of the Special Education Fund.
We further recommended that the municipal government may allocate funds
for public school expenses from the General Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) provide the necessary
support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own MRFs and to implement
waste segregation at source; (b) strictly enforce and intensify the information
campaign on the no-littering policy among the residents; and (c) consider embarking
on effective projects and programs to support the LGUs ecological solid waste
management program; (d) strictly imposed penalty to those households who refuse to
segregate the non and biodegradable waste materials; and (e) strictly comply with all
other prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003.

We recommended for the adoption of a decentralized solid waste management
system by providing financial and technical assistance for the establishment/
operation of functional and standard-based MRFs in the barangays of the
Municipality. Further, we recommended that the municipality may initially pilot a
single barangay to establish MRF project in order to institute a system of knowledge
in establishing one as well as to gain commitment/participation of barangay officials
on the proper administration and operation of standard based MRF.

To achieve on-time submission of accounts, we recommended that the
Treasury and Accountant be directed to promptly turn-over documents, reports and
journals under their possession on the prescribed time which shall be duly covered by
transmittal letters, in turn, cause their prompt submission for audit. In addition, said
transmittal letters shall prove the compliance of each to their duties over the agency’s
accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Require the Municipal Accountant to record as asset all procurements of equipment, machineries, furniture and fixtures. 2. Require the Supply Officer to make sure that all procurements of equipment and machineries accompanied with Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment by the end user for monitoring, control and accountability. 3. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to take up the understatement of the asset
observations.managementAction: The Supply Officer- Designate had issued the necessary Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to the end user for understatement of Equipment amounting to P521,713.50. Improvements, machineries additions, alterations, and etc. must be capitalized when these extend the life, increase the capacity or improve the efficiency or safety of the property. The end-user for Equipment by the end-user for monitoring, control and accountability.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the understatement of PPE amounting to P521,713.50.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to stop utilizing Special Education Fund (SEF) for wages and allowances to non-teaching personnel as it is not one of the authorized activities provided in Section 1 of Republic Act No. 5447 and Item No. 2.3 of DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01-A dated March 14, 2000. 2. Direct the Local School Board to monitor the utilization of financial assistance released to various public schools and district offices and make sure that all assets procured under SEF are accounted for in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Other Implemented: payment for other allowances to non-teaching personnel were stopped.
observations.reason: The LGU stopped the payment of wages/allowances to non-teaching personnel charged to SEF. Monitoring of utilization of financial assistance to public schools and district offices remains to be ensured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management that the LGU: 1. Demand from SOLSED the submission of documents required mentioned in Section 4 of COA Circular No. 2007-001. 2. Refrain from releasing the fund to any NGOs unless they comply with the required documents in violation of Section 4.4 of COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007. Also, the LGU and SOLSED failed to execute some of their obligations stated in the Memorandum of Agreement.
observations.managementAction: The LGU no longer release funds to NGOs unless there is compliance with the documents required in Section 4 of COA Circular No. 2007-001. The request for amendment/clarification and amortization coordination was to be undertaken.
observations.reason: The request for write-off of accounts is to be discussed in the succeeding Sangguniang Bayan Sessions. SOLSED did not execute some obligations; goal and objectives of the program not attained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the maintenance of Special account for BOMWASA to determine whether the income generated from this public utility was sufficient to meet its operating cost. Also require proper accounting in consonance with Section 105 of the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan Members of LGU Bontoc decided not to declare BOMWASA as an Economic Enterprise.
observations.reason: Management stated BOMWASA will not be declared as Economic Enterprise; however this affects the LGU’s ability to determine operating cost coverage.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should exert extra effort to prove the existence of the above cited accounts and if effort proved futile, a resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan should be passed to apply for the write-off of the accounts only upon prior authority from the COA.
observations.managementAction: These accounts were already included in the Executive agenda for the next Sangguniang Bayan Session to apply for write-off of the accounts. The accounts are to be validated and supporting schedules to be prepared.
observations.reason: Request for Sangguniang Bayan Resolution to apply for write-off of accounts could not be located; further validation pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE: 1. Instruct the Inventory Committee to commence the conduct of the annual physical inventory of all LGU properties. 2. Cause the Inventory Committee to assign property numbers on all LGU properties and equipment and issue ARE to accountable persons issued properties and equipment.
observations.managementAction: Physical Count is not yet through. A Committee commenced the physical count of properties. The Supplier Officer is designated and monitoring initiated.
observations.reason: The physical inventory is not yet finished. The Supplier Officer is newly designated and the inventory is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Require the employees concerned to adhere strictly to Section 89 of P.D. 1445; COA Circular 97-002 specifically Sections 5.7 and 8, and COA Circular 2012-004 dtd. November 28, 2012 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. 2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant-designate to send demand letters to accountable officers who were granted cash advances for the liquidation thereof.
observations.managementAction: Implemented: Discontinuance of payment and demand letters were issued. Employees were directed to liquidate cash advances as per COA circulars. Some cash advances were liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly adhere to Section 287 of RA 7160 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 and ensure that only projects aligned with the purpose/objectives of the 20% Development Fund be funded from such appropriation.
observations.managementAction: Implemented: some projects were transferred to General Fund; awareness raised among staff. Municipal Accountant agreed to transfer perennial programs to General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity shall: 1. Require the BAC to demand from all department/offices to submit PPMPs to the BAC secretariat for review and consolidation into the proposed APP. 2. Require the Requisitioning Office concerned to submit PPMPs with provisions for foreseeable emergencies based on historical records. 3. Require the BAC Secretariat to prepare the APP once the Proposed Budget for the following year is approved and require review and update of individual PPMPs and the APP regularly.
observations.managementAction: Implemented: BAC to collect PPMPs and prepare APP; requisitioning offices to include contingencies; review and updating procedures in place.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: 1. Cause the discontinuance of the payment of reimbursement for fuel expenses to the Vice Mayor and all SB Members. 2. All fuel expenses of the SB office must be supplied by Local Supplier contracted by LGU Bontoc. 3. Instruct the Municipal Accountant that all official travels must be supported with Driver’s Trip Tickets duly approved by proper officials. These tickets must be properly filled-up and must identify the government vehicle used.
observations.managementAction: Implemented: the practice was stopped. Instructed that fuel expenses be supplied via contracted supplier and proper documentation be used for official travels.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the officials authorized to receive monthly RATA to comply with Budget Circular No. 103 which took effect on January 1, 2013 and cause the Municipal Accountant and the official who is in-charge in the preparation of the payroll for RATA to attach in the payroll a certified list of officers who are collecting RATA indicating the number of days they have rendered actual service, information as to whether there is leave of absence incurred and what kind of leave, and for Sanggunian members a certified list indicating percentage of sessions attended.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented: RATA procedures were improved and some supporting documents attached in payrolls. Compliance to Budget Circular No. 103 was initiated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant observe strictly COA Circular No. 2012-001 and that all fund transfers must have a Memorandum of Agreement to include a provision that the infrastructure project must be validated upon completion of the project through the preparation of an inspection report by the Municipal Engineer based on the statement of work accomplished prepared by the barangay/school to be attached to the liquidation report.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented: Treasurer explained that liquidation reports were submitted but inadvertently attached; subsequent Financial Assistance will not be granted if previous FAs were not liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that seminars/trainings where participants come from the province should be conducted within the province in order to minimize the cost of the conduct of these activities and save funds for other relative projects.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented: management to exercise prudence in the usage of government funds and ensure seminars conducted within area of concern when possible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify collection strategies to intensify collection of RPT taxes and set realizable target for CY 2015, intensify collection and intensify collection measures.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented: collection strategies were intensified; collection improved though target not fully realized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee comply with all the requirements of RA 9184, its implementing rules and all laws, decrees, circulars, memoranda and issuances governing infrastructure procurement. Specifically: a) ensure contracts are entered into and mobilization payments supported with contracts; b) ensure detailed engineering surveys and designs are conducted and attached; c) utilize PhilGEPS; d) include arbitration process; e) ensure procurement outputs are adequate for procurement at hand; and others as enumerated.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented: BAC to comply with requirements; some contracts revised and procedures strengthened.
observations.reason: Some BAC practices improved but full compliance with all documentary requirements and PhilGEPS utilization remains to be achieved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that JO contracts should contain statements of duties and responsibilities or equivalent terms to serve as standards with which to base the accomplishment and ensure effectiveness, efficiency and necessity of services.
observations.managementAction: Implemented: contracts were revised to incorporate duties and responsibilities and standard performance measures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that whenever medicines were procured, management should strictly adhere to EO No. 49, DOH AO No. 163 series of 2002 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to ensure items procured originated from legitimate sources and are effective and genuine. If drugs are not in PNDF, a written approval of the head of National Drug Policy Staff should be attached in the disbursement voucher. Further, the Property Custodian of the RHU should submit Monthly Report of Medicines Issued.
observations.managementAction: Implemented: procurement procedures for medicines were adjusted to conform to regulations; controls strengthened and documentation required.
